series_metadata.series_description: The lost time claim file contains information pertaining to a claimant’s claim for workers’ compensation benefits, such as, but not limited to: medical records, requests for treatment, requests for payment of compensation, compensation payment records, and orders issued by BWC or the Industrial Commission. A lost time claim is a claim in which compensation benefits have been paid, such as, but not limited to: temporary total disability compensation, salary continuation, permanent partial disability compensation, or permanent total disability compensation. The statute of limitations for lost time claims is as follows: •For claims with DOI prior to 8/25/06, the claim expires ten (10) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, or date of death. •For claims with DOI from 8/25/06 to 9/14/20, the claim expires five (5) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, or date of death. •For claims with DOI on or after 9/15/2020, the claim expires five (5) years from the date of the last medical service rendered, compensation payment, or date of death.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Paper: lost time claim file claim has reached appropriate statute of limitations (i.e. expiration date) according date of injury (DOI) year. Digitally image claim file records, 30 days quality control, and ; Electronic: lost time claim file claim has reached appropriate statute of limitations (i.e. expiration date) according date of injury (DOI) year. ; Microform: lost time claim file claim has reached appropriate statute of limitations (i.e. expiration date) according date of injury (DOI) year
retention_rules.disposition_method: Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Delete ; Microform: Burn
